zh.lifeSummary: 當John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II於1397年出生在蘇格蘭貝維其可爾波爾瓦特時，他的父親John de St Clair - Laird of Polwarth I35歲，母親Elizabeth de Polwarth32歲。他和Isobel de Home至少有8個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1466年12月20日在他的家鄉享年69歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  French: habitational name from any of the places so called, for example in Normandy, named from a religious dedication to a (male) St. Clair.
2  North American variant spelling of   St  .   Claire  .

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: St
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

nameMeanings.name: Clair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the male personal name Clair (from Latin Clarus ‘illustrious’), which was borne by two early Christian saints, a 3rd-century bishop of Nantes and the 4th-century St. Clair of Aquitaine.
French: nickname for a cheerful individual, from the adjective clair ‘bright, light’. Compare Leclair 1.
American shortened form of French   Leclair   1, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Leclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Emile, Normand, Gaston, Adrien, Antoine, Lucien, Monique, Philias, Camille.

French: nickname for a cheerful individual, a variant of Clair 2 ‘bright, light’, with fused masculine definite article le. Derivation from the male personal name Clair 1 is also possible. Compare Laclair , Laclaire , and Leclear .
French: altered form of   Leclerc   1.

variants.name: Klar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ezriel, Tzvi, Baruch, Devorah, Isadore, Nachman, Yitzchok.  German Gerhard, Johann, Erhard, Kurt, Willibald.
German: nickname from Middle High German and Middle Low German klār ‘pure, beautiful, clear’.
German: from the female personal name Klara, an equivalent of Clara (see Clair and Sinclair ).

variants.name: Cler
meanings: French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Clarin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luz, Alejandro, Armida, Epifano, Florencio, Jose, Libia, Librada, Maria Luisa, Raul, Santos, Sergio.

Swedish: variant of Klarin, ornamental or perhaps habitational name formed with klar ‘clear’ (possibly representing an element of a placename) + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Clair  .

variants.name: Auclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Raoul, Andre, Fernand, Ovila, Alphonse, Cecile, Gaston, Germaine, Jacques, Jean-Paul.
French: patronymic from Clair , from (fils) au Clair ‘(son) of Clair’. Compare Leclerc 2 and Oclair .

History: Pierre and André Auclair, two brothers from Saint-Vivien near La Rochelle in France, are recorded in Charlesbourg, QC, in 1679 and 1681, respectively, the years of their marriage. The descendants of one or another brother also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Leclerc, Oclair, and O'Clair.

nameMeanings.name: Laird
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: status name for a landlord or landowner, from northern Middle English laverd ‘lord’.

variants.name: Lard
meanings: Scottish: altered form of   Laird  .

variants.name: Leard
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Laird  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II was born in 1397, in Polwarth, Berwickshire, Scotland, his father, John de St Clair - Laird of Polwarth I, was 35 and his mother, Elizabeth de Polwarth, was 32. He had at least 8 sons and 5 daughters with Isobel de Home. He died on 20 December 1466, in his hometown, at the age of 69.

Il a eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles avec Isobel de Home. Il est décédé le 20 décembre 1466, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans.

Er hatte mit Isobel de Home mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 20. Dezember 1466 im Alter von 69 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

ja.lifeSummary: John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth IIが1397年にスコットランドバーウィクシュアイアポルワースで生まれた時，父John de St Clair - Laird of Polwarth Iは35歳，母Elizabeth de Polwarthは32歳であった。彼はIsobel de Homeとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼は1466年12月20日に故郷で69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II(이)가 1397년 스컷랜드 버윅실르 포워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de St Clair - Laird of Polwarth I(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Polwarth(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Isobel de Home(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1466년 12월 20일 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II родился в 1397 г. в местности полварт, бирвиккшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John de St Clair - Laird of Polwarth I было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Polwarth, 32. У него было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей от Isobel de Home. Он умер 20 декабря 1466 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет.
